name: Biscotti Di Pratto
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. On a baking sheet, roast blanched and unblanched almonds until the blanched almonds start to brown; shake pan occasionally for 5 to 8 minutes. Remove blanched almonds and place in blender container, grinding until it turns into powder. Set aside. Return whole almonds to oven for 2 to 3 minutes longer. Remove and chop whole almonds roughly with a knife, leaving large chunks. Set aside.
3. Turn oven down to 300 degrees. Grease baking sheet and dust with flour; set aside.
4. In a small bowl, beat 3 eggs, vanilla, and almond extract.
5. In a large bowl, mix together flour, sugar, baking soda, salt, and ground almonds. Make a well in the center and add the eggs, blending gradually to incorporate all of the flour mixture. The dough should be stiff.
6. If it is too stiff to hold together, add a little water. Add the crushed almonds and knead them evenly into the dough. Divide dough into 3 portions.
7. Form each portion into a long log, rounded or slightly oval (about 1 1/2 inches in diameter). It will flatten as it bakes.
8. Place logs on prepared baking sheet. Bake for 45 to 50 minutes. Remove from oven and let rest for 5 minutes.
9. Turn oven down to 275 degrees. With a serrated knife, cut logs into 3/4-inch slices (NOTE: Unlike slicing a loaf of bread straight across its width, cut biscotti on a 45-degree angle). Lay slices flat on 2 baking sheets. Return to oven for 20 to 25 minutes, turning slices halfway through the baking time. Remove from oven; allow to cool completely before sealing in containers. They will stay fresh up to 1 month.
